title: On entropy Marton-type inequalities and small symmetric differences with cosets of abelian groups

thm:1.1.title: GGMT2 Theorem 1.1
thm:1.1.summary: PFR covering theorem: If $A \subseteq G$ ($m$-torsion abelian) with $|A+A| \le K|A|$, then $A$ is covered by at most $(2K)^{O(m^3)}$ cosets of a subgroup $H$ with $|H| \le |A|$.

def:1.3.summary: Distributional $\varepsilon$-approximate group: $A \subseteq G$ where proportion of $(a,b) \in A \times A$ with $a+b \in U$ is $\ge 1-\varepsilon$ for some $|U| = |A|$.

thm:1.5.summary: Main theorem: If $A$ is a distributional $\varepsilon$-approximate group, then $|A \Delta (H+x)|/|H| \le 240 \cdot \log(\varepsilon^{-1}|A|) \cdot \varepsilon$ for some coset $H+x$. Sharp up to log factor.

lem:2.1.summary: Entropy lower bound: $H(X+Y) \ge \max(H(X), H(Y))$ for independent $X, Y$.

prop:2.2.title: GGMT2 Theorem 1.3
prop:2.2.summary: Entropic PFR in $m$-torsion: For $X,Y$ in $m$-torsion $G$, exists subgroup $H$ with $d(X,U_H) \le Cm^3 \cdot d(X,Y)$ and $d(Y,U_H) \le Cm^3 \cdot d(X,Y)$.

prop:2.3.title: GMT Proposition 1.3
prop:2.3.summary: Small Ruzsa distance implies coset structure: If $d(X,Y) \le \gamma$, then $d(X,U_H) \le 12 \cdot d(X,Y)$ for some subgroup $H$. Key tool from GMT (Sumsets and entropy revisited).

cor:2.4.summary: Small entropy doubling implies Ruzsa closeness to subgroup: If $\Delta H(A) \le \gamma$, then $d(U_A, U_H) \le 12 \cdot \Delta H(A)$.

lem:3.1.summary: Distributional approximate group implies small entropy doubling: If $A$ is $\varepsilon$-approximate, then $\Delta H(A) \le 2\varepsilon \cdot \log(\varepsilon^{-1}|A|)$.

lem:3.2.summary: Ruzsa closeness implies symmetric difference bound: If $d(U_A, U_H) \le \alpha < 1/10$, then $|A \Delta (H+x)| \le 10\alpha|H|$ for some $x$.

edges.note: GGMT entropic PFR specializes to $X=Y$ case for entropy doubling.
GMT small distance result gives the $d(U_A, U_H) \le 12 \cdot \Delta H(A)$ bound.
The main theorem uses Lemma 3.1 to translate the distributional approximate-group hypothesis into an entropy-doubling bound.
The main theorem uses Corollary 2.4 to convert entropy doubling into Ruzsa distance to a subgroup.
The main theorem uses Lemma 3.2 to convert Ruzsa distance into a symmetric difference with a coset.
The GMT small-distance result uses the basic entropy lower bound of Lemma 2.1.
